steps.title: Expand the right side
steps.content: Distribute \(a\) on the right: \(9x + 5 = ax + ab\). Rearranging gives \((9 - a)x = ab - 5\).

steps.title: Apply the no-solutions condition
steps.content:
A linear equation \(cx = d\) has no solutions when \(c = 0\) and \(d 
eq 0\) (the equation becomes \(0 = d\), a contradiction). So we need \(9 - a = 0\) and \(ab - 5 
eq 0\), which gives \(a = 9\) and \(9b 
eq 5\), i.e. \(b 
eq \dfrac{5}{9}\).

steps.title: Evaluate each statement
steps.content: Statement I (\(a = 9\)): required — TRUE. Statement II (\(b = 5\)): not required; any \(b \neq \dfrac{5}{9}\) works — FALSE. Statement III (\(b \neq \dfrac{5}{9}\)): required — TRUE.

steps.title: Select the answer
steps.content:
Only Statements I and III must be true.
  • A (None): Wrong; both I and III are necessary.
  • B (I only): Incomplete; III is also required.
  • C (I and II only): II is not necessary; \(b\) can be any value other than \(\dfrac{5}{9}\).
  • D (I and III only): \(a = 9\) and \(b \neq \dfrac{5}{9}\) are both required for no solutions to exist. That matches choice D — D is correct.
